commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a channels/imessage WARN before the existing private API bridge throw, covers that failure path with an iMessage action test, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main reaches the unavailable private API branch and throws without a logger call. The PR body adds after-fix terminal proof from the built action module, but I did not run a live macOS gateway restart.
commands.review_followup: No repair job is needed: the patch has no concrete code defect for automation, but maintainers should refresh checks and handle review/landing for the protected-label PR.

target.title: fix(imessage): WARN-log when private API bridge is unavailable
target.author: omarshahine

commands.command: re-review — rebased onto current main, full repo build redone, and after-fix proof now exercises `imessagemessageactions.handleaction(...)` from the built `dist/` chunk (same module the gateway loads). the real probe runs against a bogus `clipath`, returns `available:false`, and the patched assertion routes the new warn through the real `createsubsystemlogger("channels/imessage")` before the unchanged throw. captured terminal output is in the updated pr body's *evidence after fix* block.
commands.freeform_prompt: re-review — rebased onto current main, full repo build redone, and after-fix proof now exercises `imessageMessageActions.handleAction(...)` from the BUILT `dist/` chunk (same module the gateway loads). The real probe runs against a bogus `cliPath`, returns `available:false`, and the patched assertion routes the new WARN through the real `createSubsystemLogger("channels/imessage")` before the unchanged throw. Captured terminal output is in the updated PR body's *Evidence after fix* block.
